No Poverty.reason: Discusses 'Natural Disasters', which links to building resilience for the poor and vulnerable against shocks (Target 1.5).

Good Health and Well-being.reason: The topic 'Accidents' has a direct, though general, link to Target 3.6, which aims to reduce deaths and injuries from road traffic accidents.

Quality Education.reason: As a university course, it provides tertiary education (Target 4.3) and fosters an 'international perspective', promoting global citizenship (Target 4.7).

Decent Work and Economic Growth.reason: The course covers 'Business and Economy', which is central to economic growth. Language skills also contribute to employment capabilities (Target 8.5).

Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure.reason: Includes a specific topic on 'Technology', which relates to upgrading technological capabilities and innovation (Target 9.5).

Reduced Inequalities.reason: Topics like 'Politics' and 'Judicial Process' could inferentially address social inclusion and equal opportunity (Targets 10.2, 10.3), but it is not a stated focus.

Sustainable Cities and Communities.reason: The topic 'Natural Disasters' directly relates to Target 11.5 (reduce deaths and economic losses from disasters). 'Accidents' relates to road safety (Target 11.2).

Climate Action.reason: The topic 'Natural Disasters' directly addresses Target 13.1, which focuses on strengthening resilience and adaptive capacity to climate-related hazards and natural disasters.

Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions.reason: Core topics include 'Judicial Process in Thailand' and 'Crime', directly supporting Target 16.3 (rule of law) and 16.1 (reduce violence).

Partnerships for the Goals.reason: The course fosters an 'international perspective', which very broadly supports the spirit of global partnership and knowledge sharing (Target 17.16).
